_meta.title: Hand-drawn field sketch extraction — George Bouchles (26-018)
_meta.description: Structured data extracted only from legible annotations on sketch_for_claude.jpg. Illegible or unlabeled values left null to avoid hallucination.

observations: Structures labeled: 'House' (center), 'GAR' (garage, right, underlined), and a drilled well top-right labeled '23' + well symbol + 'DRILLED'.
Well offsets dimensioned off the garage/property corner: '23' (horizontal leg) and '30.5' (vertical leg). No explicit 'field-to-well' distance is labeled.
Circled '21"' inside the disposal field — clearly inch-marked, read as a 21-inch depth (likely depth to limiting factor); placed in depth_measurements.
Disposal-field polygon dimensions (feet, unlabeled as to type): 28.5, 30' (with a '(4)' note), 16.5, 27, 17, 51.5, 78, and 11.
A '55' annotation with '9' over '2.5' (reads like 55 and a 9/2.5 ratio) at the field's left side — unit/meaning not stated.
Two small boxes inside the field marked 'T' (test pits) and additional small boxes/marks ('B'/'D'); a partially legible curved label near center (~'EAR') could not be read confidently.
Module/fill label near the field reads approximately 'MP 20' with hatching — could not confirm a brand/model, so module_type left null.

Separate small box at upper-left (outside the field) annotated '21.5', '21', and '3 1/4' — appears to be a soil/test-pit profile note, but units (depth vs. dimension) are not stated, so not assigned to depth_measurements.

Lower-left detail box (the apparent elevation/benchmark reference) shows pencil numbers ('25'?, '9', '0') and a red note '+6"' with a red arrow. No ERP description text is legible, so all elevation_data fields left null; the '+6"' is noted here only.
No soil colors, soil textures, or named limiting factors (e.g., ledge, water table, mottling) are written legibly anywhere on the sketch.
